hypothesis: If AMDEN uses an EGNN-based diffusion model with VE/VP SDEs (and optional energy-based noise prediction with HMC refinement), then it can generate amorphous materials across different thermal histories and multi-element compositions whose RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions closely match MD-simulated references, with the energy-based HMC refinement producing closer agreement than standard denoising and classifier-free guidance enabling control of Young's modulus.
